sourceMovie.title: Five Nights at Freddy's
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Five Nights at Freddy's (2023) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Emma Tammi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Insidious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Five Nights at Freddy's clicked for you, Insidious (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Five Nights at Freddy's?
faq.answer: Try Insidious, Case 39, Thir13en Ghosts—each shares DNA with Five Nights at Freddy's while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Five Nights at Freddy's?
faq.answer: Five Nights at Freddy's (2023) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Five Nights at Freddy's part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Five Nights at Freddy's Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
